snippets.title: Minimal PyTorch MLP for XOR
snippets.code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

# XOR dataset
X = torch.tensor([[0., 0.], [0., 1.], [1., 0.], [1., 1.]])
y = torch.tensor([[0.], [1.], [1.], [0.]])

class XorMLP(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(2, 2),   # hidden layer: 2 units
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(2, 1),   # output logit
            nn.Sigmoid(),
        )

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.net(x)

model = XorMLP()
loss_fn = nn.BCELoss()
opt = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.5)

for step in range(5000):
    opt.zero_grad()
    pred = model(X)
    loss = loss_fn(pred, y)
    loss.backward()
    opt.step()

print(model(X).round())  # expect ~[[0],[1],[1],[0]]
snippets.explanation: Two hidden ReLU units give enough nonlinear feature recombination to fit XOR — impossible for a single linear threshold unit on the raw inputs.

snippets.title: Configurable classification MLP
snippets.code:
import torch.nn as nn

def make_mlp(d_in: int, hidden: list[int], d_out: int) -> nn.Sequential:
    layers = []
    prev = d_in
    for h in hidden:
        layers += [nn.Linear(prev, h), nn.ReLU()]
        prev = h
    layers.append(nn.Linear(prev, d_out))  # logits; softmax in loss or inference
    return nn.Sequential(*layers)

model = make_mlp(d_in=784, hidden=[128, 64], d_out=10)
# CrossEntropyLoss applies log-softmax internally on 10 logits
snippets.explanation: The hidden list defines depth and width per hidden tier; the final Linear emits class logits without activation when using nn.CrossEntropyLoss.

snippets.title: Inspecting hidden activations (debugging boundaries)
snippets.code:
import torch

model = make_mlp(2, [4], 1)
grid = torch.stack([
    torch.tensor([x, y], dtype=torch.float32)
    for x in torch.linspace(0, 1, 20)
    for y in torch.linspace(0, 1, 20)
])

# Hook to capture last hidden layer output before final Linear
hidden_out = []
def hook(module, inp, out):
    hidden_out.append(out.detach())

handle = model[1].register_forward_hook(hook)  # after first ReLU block
with torch.no_grad():
    logits = model(grid)
handle.remove()

print(hidden_out[0].shape)  # (400, 4) — 4 hidden features per grid point
snippets.explanation: Visualizing hidden activations across an input grid shows how each hidden unit carves regions of the plane — the building blocks of the non-linear output boundary.
